perturbation_note: instrument_gap=1 marks GOES/ACE data gaps or sensor calibration periods. Standard Cosmo stress test for T6.
fragility_global_note: Solar data is highly reliable but instrument changes between GOES/SDO generations create step-changes. Use homogenised series where available. Annotate all instrument transitions as U(t) perturbation events.

columns.fragility_note: International sunspot number v2 is well-calibrated. Legacy v1 records need conversion.
columns.description: Smoothed monthly international sunspot number (solar organisational activity).

columns.fragility_note: Kp is a global average; local geomagnetic effects differ significantly.
columns.description: Monthly maximum Kp geomagnetic index (inverted: low Kp = high magnetic resilience). Invert: R = 1 − Kp_norm.

columns.fragility_note: F10.7 is measured daily at Penticton; highly consistent since 1947.
columns.description: Solar radio flux F10.7 (proxy for multi-wavelength emission coherence / integration).

columns.fragility_note: Flare index definition varies; use GOES X-ray background flux for consistency.
columns.description: Cumulative solar flare energy index above background (symbolic stock: persistent structured signal).

columns.fragility_note: ap index is the linear-scale version of Kp; consistent since 1932.
columns.description: Monthly mean ap geomagnetic activity index (environmental demand on Earth's magnetosphere).

data_preparation_notes: Recommended source: NOAA SWPC monthly values (ftp://ftp.swpc.noaa.gov/pub/indices/old_indices/)
cumulative_flare_index: compute as rolling sum of GOES X-ray class-weighted events over 6 months.
Annotate instrument transitions (GOES-15 → GOES-16, Jan 2017) as instrument_gap=1.
Minimum 1 full solar cycle (~11 years = 132 monthly rows). Recommended: 2+ cycles.
F10.7: use adjusted values (not observed) for long-term trend analyses.
